none: Ocean dumping criteria, Proposed revisions : environmental impact statement.
Ocean dumping criteria, Proposed revisions : environmental impact statement.
Federal Agencies; Director, Oil and Special Materials Control Division (OSMCD); National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA); Department of the Corps of Engineers (USACE)

This project, initiated in 1973, aimed to assess the feasibility of utilizing ocean dumping as a method for managing industrial waste in the Gulf of Mexico. Recognizing the growing volume of waste and the limited availability of alternative disposal methods, the EPA initiated a study to evaluate the environmental impacts and economic viability of ocean dumping. However, due to concerns about potential ecological damage and the lack of conclusive evidence supporting its long-term sustainability, the project was ultimately halted in 1977, with the EPA opting for a more targeted approach focused on improved waste management practices and the development of new technologies for treating and disposing of industrial waste more effectively. Therefore, the project was cancelled.
